title: The Ultimate Guide to 8 Herbal Creams for Oily Hair

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for oily hair because it helps to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reduce excess oil, and prevent clogged pores.
Cineole is good for oily hair because it helps to reduce oil production and purify the scalp, allowing hair to stay cleaner for longer.
Quercetin is good for oily hair because it helps control oil production and reduce inflammation in the scalp, resulting in a balanced and healthy hair growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a popular ingredient in hair care products. The oil is extracted from the leaves of the Melaleuca plant and has been used for centuries for its antibacterial and antifungal properties. When added to hair creams, it can help control oily hair by reducing the amount of oil produced by the scalp. It does this by balancing the scalp's pH levels and killing bacteria that can cause oil glands to become clogged. This results in a reduction in oil production, which can lead to less greasy hair. Additionally, tea tree oil has antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to oily hair. By using Melaleuca alternifolia creams, people with oily hair can find relief from the constant struggle of dealing with greasy locks. The oil's ability to reduce oil production and soothe the scalp makes it a popular ingredient in hair care products designed for oily hair.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for oily hair, start by gathering 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax. Next, add 10 drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the mixture. In a small saucepan, melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax over low heat, stirring occasionally until smooth. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before stirring in the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil. Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool completely before use. Once cooled, apply a small amount of the cream to your scalp and work it into your hair, focusing on the oily areas. The Eucalyptus globulus oil in the cream will help to balance your scalp's oil production, reducing dandruff and itchiness while leaving your hair feeling clean and refreshed.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for oily hair

Caryophyllene is good for oily hair because it has antifungal properties that help control the growth of scalp yeast, which can contribute to oiliness and flakiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams can help with oily hair because of its soothing and balancing properties. The lavender oil in the cream has antiseptic and antifungal properties that can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair. It also has a calming effect on the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can lead to excess oil production. Additionally, the cream can help to regulate the pH balance of the scalp, which can help to control oil production and prevent greasiness. The cream also moisturizes the scalp, without clogging the hair follicles, allowing the hair to stay clean and healthy-looking for longer. This helps to reduce the appearance of oily hair and leaves the hair feeling soft and looking shiny.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for oily hair, start by gathering a few ingredients. You'll need dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a small amount of water. Begin by mixing 2 tablespoons of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small saucepan, and heat the mixture over low heat for about 10 minutes, allowing the Eucalyptus to infuse into the oil. Next, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the leaves. In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of the infused oil over low heat. Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly add in 1 tablespoon of water, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool and solidify. Once set, the Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream is ready to use. To apply, simply massage a small amount into your scalp to help control oily hair and reduce dandruff.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream, also known as rosemary essential oil-based cream, is a popular hair treatment for oily hair. This is because rosemary has natural properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The herb's active compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, work to reduce sebum buildup on the hair and scalp. By doing so, it prevents excessive oiliness and greasiness. Additionally, rosemary oil has antimicrobial properties, which help to keep the scalp clean and free from dirt and bacteria that can contribute to oiliness. The cream's cooling and soothing effects can also calm an itchy scalp, reducing the urge to scratch and further stimulate oil production. By applying rosemary cream to the scalp, it can help to regulate oil production, reduce oiliness, and promote a healthy scalp and hair.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream that helps with oily hair, you will need a few ingredients and some simple equipment. First, you will need to dry and powder 2-3 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus leaves. This can be done by tying the leaves in a bunch, hanging them upside down in a warm place, and allowing them to dry completely. Once the leaves are dry, simply grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix the powdered Eucalyptus with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has reached a smooth consistency. Remove the saucepan from the heat and allow the mixture to cool and thicken. Once it has cooled, transfer the mixture to a small jar or container and store it in the fridge. To use, simply massage a small amount of the cream into your scalp and hair, focusing on the oily areas. The Eucalyptus globulus will help to balance the oil production in your scalp, reducing oiliness and leaving your hair feeling clean and refreshed.

Geraniol is good for oily hair because it helps to reduce sebum production and balance the scalp's natural oil levels, leaving hair feeling cleaner and looking healthier.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, has a calming and soothing effect on the scalp. When used in a hair cream, its properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation that can lead to oily hair. The cream also has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which prevent the growth of microorganisms that can cause oiliness and other scalp problems. Furthermore, lemongrass oil in the cream helps to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing excessive oiliness and promoting a healthy scalp environment. Additionally, its astringent properties help to tighten the hair follicles, reducing oil secretion and giving a cleaner and fresher look to the hair.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for oily hair, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves, which you can purchase from a herbal store or online. You'll also need a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and a moisturizing cream or lotion base. To start, infuse the dried Eucalyptus leaves in a carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and covering them with oil. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, until the oil has taken on the herb's scent and properties. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a moisturizing cream or lotion base, and stir well to combine. Finally, transfer the cream to a clean jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Apply the cream to your scalp or hair as needed to help control oil production and reduce dandruff, while also leaving your hair feeling soft and manageable.

Flavonoids are good for oily hair because they help to balance the scalp's natural oils and reduce inflammation, leading to a healthier and more balanced hair growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is good for oily hair because it helps control excess oil production. Aloe vera has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that clean the scalp and remove dirt and oil buildup. This helps keep the scalp healthy and free from irritation. When applied to oily hair, aloe vera cream absorbs extra oil and reduces its appearance, giving hair a cleaner and healthier look. The cream also has soothing and calming effects that can help reduce dandruff and itchiness, making it a great solution for people with oily and sensitive scalps. Additionally, aloe vera is rich in vitamins and minerals that nourish and moisturize hair, leaving it soft and manageable. By balancing oil production and hydrating hair, aloe barbadensis cream helps maintain a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for oily hair, start by infusing dried eucalyptus leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1 cup of dried eucalyptus leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every few days to help the leaves release their oils into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new container, discarding the eucalyptus leaves. Next, mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of a natural emollient like shea butter or beeswax, and 1 tablespoon of a natural preservative like vitamin E oil. Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring until the ingredients are fully combined and the cream has a smooth, spreadable consistency. Allow the cream to cool before transferring it to a clean container. This eucalyptus globulus herbal cream can be applied directly to the scalp to help control oily hair and promote a healthy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis cream is a good product for oily hair because it helps to control the production of oil. The cream contains natural ingredients that balance the scalp's pH level, reducing the amount of oil produced. This cream also has antiseptic properties, which help to clean the scalp and remove dirt and impurities that can clog pores and make hair look greasier. By regulating oil production and cleaning the scalp, Saponaria officinalis cream keeps the hair looking healthy and clean for a longer period. It also helps to reduce dandruff and flaking, which can contribute to an oily appearance. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to soften and smooth the hair, making it easier to manage and style.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream that helps with oily hair, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, gather some dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves, which have natural antiseptic and decongestant properties that can help control oily hair. You'll also need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, aloe vera gel, and a small amount of beeswax to thicken the cream. Start by making a tea by steeping the dried Eucalyptus leaves in boiling water, then straining and cooling the liquid. Next, mix the cooled Eucalyptus tea with the carrier oil, aloe vera gel, and beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has thickened into a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool before transferring it to a clean container. Once cooled, your Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream is ready to use – simply apply it to your scalp and massage it in before washing your hair as usual to help control oiliness and keep your locks looking healthy and balanced.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a natural ingredient that has been used for centuries to solve hair problems. The cream made from thyme is good for oily hair because it has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help to control oil production. This means that the cream can reduce the amount of oil your hair produces, making it look healthier and cleaner for longer. The cream also has a cooling effect that can calm the scalp, which is often irritated by oily hair. Additionally, thyme cream has antifungal properties that can prevent the growth of fungus and bacteria that can make oily hair worse. When applied to the hair and scalp, thyme cream can help to balance the pH level, making it an effective solution for oily hair.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream to help with oily hair, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need to make an infusion of Eucalyptus globulus leaves by steeping a handful of the leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let the mixture cool down and then strain it to remove the leaves. Next, you'll need to mix the cooled infusion with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:10. After that, you'll need to add a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to give the cream its desired consistency. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, then slowly add it to the oil-infusion mixture while stirring constantly. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, transfer it to a container and store it in the fridge. To use the cream, simply apply it to your scalp and massage it in before washing your hair as usual. The eucalyptus globulus will help to reduce oil production and balance your scalp's natural pH.

Tannins are good for oily hair because they have natural astringent properties that help balance the scalp's oil production and reduce greasiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a popular choice for oily hair because it helps to control oil production in the scalp. The menthol in eucalyptus globulus acts as a natural astringent, which tightens the pores on the scalp and reduces oil secretion. This results in a decrease in greasiness and oiliness, making it easier to manage oily hair. The cream also has a cooling effect, which can help to reduce inflammation and itchiness in the scalp. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has antifungal properties, which can help to prevent scalp infections that can contribute to oily hair. When applied to the hair, the eucalyptus globulus cream can help to balance the scalp's natural pH, creating an environment that is less conducive to oil production. This makes it easier to keep oily hair clean and manageable, reducing the need for frequent washing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams that help with oily hair, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, gather 1 cup of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax. Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring well after each heating. Once melted, add the dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves to the mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours, allowing the leaves to release their oil and fragrance. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a glass jar or container. This Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream can be applied to the scalp to help control oil production, reduce dandruff, and promote a healthy scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for oily hair

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be beneficial for oily hair. Some herbs that can help are Melaleuca alternifolia and Lavandula angustifolia. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can cause oiliness. It also has antifungal properties, which can help prevent fungal infections that can lead to oily hair.  Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender oil, has a soothing effect on the scalp and can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to oiliness. It also has antiseptic properties that can help clean and purify the scalp.  Using herbal creams with these herbs can help regulate the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the scalp, and reduce oiliness. This can improve the health and appearance of the hair, making it look cleaner and more manageable. Fixing oily hair issues is important because it can affect self-confidence and daily life. Oily hair can be difficult to style and can make people feel self-conscious about their appearance. By using herbal creams to control oiliness, people can feel more confident and comfortable in their own skin, which can lead to a better quality of life. Improving oily hair can also save time and money that is usually spent on styling products and frequent hair washing.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal creams that can help remedy oily hair is Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree oil), Cymbopogon citratus (lemongrass oil), and Aloe barbadensis (aloe vera). Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair. Lemongrass oil has a stimulating effect that increases blood flow to the scalp, helping to balance the production of oil. Aloe vera is a natural moisturizer that helps to keep the scalp hydrated, reducing the need for excess oil production. This combination works well because it addresses the root causes of oily hair - bacterial growth and imbalance in oil production - while also providing moisturizing benefits to the scalp. The antibacterial properties of tea tree oil and the stimulating effect of lemongrass oil complement each other, while the moisturizing properties of aloe vera help to soothe and calm the scalp.
supplementary_avoid: If you have oily hair, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. For example, creams containing Calendula (Calendula officinalis) may not be a good choice. This is because Calendula can be too rich and heavy for oily hair, which can make it look greasier. Another herbal cream to avoid is that containing Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla). While Chamomile is known for its soothing properties, it can also add extra moisture to your hair, leading to oiliness.  You should also be cautious with creams containing Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata), as it can clog pores and exacerbate oily hair. Another herb to avoid is Witch Hazel (Hamamelis virginiana), which can be too drying for oily hair. If not used carefully, it can strip your hair of its natural oils, making it look even oilier.  Additionally, creams containing Valerian (Valeriana officinalis) should be used with caution, as it can be too rich and heavy for oily hair. Similarly, creams containing Sage (Salvia officinalis) should be avoided, as it can be too drying for oily hair.  Remember to always read the label carefully and look for creams that are specifically formulated for oily hair.
supplementary_other_remedies: Using apple cider vinegar as a final rinse after shampooing can help control oily hair. The acidity in the vinegar helps balance the pH of the scalp, reducing oil production. The vinegar also helps remove product buildup and close the cuticles of the hair, making it less prone to oiliness.  Another remedy is egg whites, which can be used as a hair mask. The protein in the egg whites helps repair and rebuild the hair structure, making it less likely to produce excess oil. This is because the protein helps fortify the hair's natural barrier, preventing oil from seeping through.  Baking soda can also be used to help control oily hair. When mixed with water, it creates a paste that can be applied to the scalp. The baking soda helps neutralize the pH of the scalp, reducing oil production. It also helps remove product buildup and clean the scalp, reducing oiliness.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with oily hair often experience skin problems like acne and blackheads. This is because the oil from their scalp can clog their pores, leading to inflammation and bacterial growth. Medicinal herbs like tea tree oil and neem can help. Tea tree oil has antiseptic properties that reduce bacterial growth and fight inflammation, while neem has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control oil production and clear pores.  Another common issue for people with oily hair is dandruff and itchy scalp. This is often caused by a fungal infection that feeds on oil and dead skin cells. Herbs like sage and rosemary can help. Sage has antifungal properties that can combat fungal infections, while rosemary has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth.  People with oily hair may also experience hair loss due to the irritation and inflammation caused by excess oil. Herbs like saw palmetto and ginseng can help. Saw palmetto blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can cause hair loss, while ginseng improves circulation and promotes healthy hair growth.  Finally, people with oily hair may experience greasy skin and body odor. Herbs like lemongrass and patchouli can help. Lemongrass has natural deodorizing properties that reduce sweat and body odor, while patchouli has antiseptic properties that help reduce bacterial growth and promote healthy skin.
